description: A refinery economist uses an LP model to decide the optimal blend of crude oils to purchase for next month to maximize gasoline production.

nodes.task_description: Gather current market prices for available crude oils (e.g., WTI, Brent, Mars).

expected_outcome.expected: A list of crudes and their current spot prices is compiled.

nodes.task_description: Input the crude prices and their assay data into the refinery's Linear Programming (LP) model.

expected_outcome.expected: The LP model is correctly populated with the latest data.

nodes.task_description: Run the LP model to determine the most profitable crude slate and generate a purchase recommendation.

expected_outcome.expected: The model outputs a recommended blend of crudes that maximizes the refinery's gross margin.
